breakdown: The PR implements correct thread-safety patterns (double-checked locking) with clear variable names, but lacks any error handling (no try/except around cache operations) and test coverage entirely; documentation is minimal with only inline bug-comments visible.
reasoning: Naming is strong across all identifiers (breakers, cache constants, parameters), but error handling is absent—no exception handling around init_schema_doc_cache() or CircuitBreaker construction, which could silently fail; type annotations are present but test coverage cannot be assessed as no tests were included in the diff.
